title: App won't start: can't read its own config file
description_md:
The `billing` web app runs as the unprivileged `billing` user. On boot it reads `/etc/billing/billing.conf` — but it's now failing with `permission denied` reading that file. Someone 'locked down' the config last night. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`) and make the config readable by the account the app runs under, without exposing the secret to every user on the box more than necessary.

You're done when the `billing` user can read `/etc/billing/billing.conf`.
